summary: This notebook serves as a tutorial demonstrating how to fine-tune and run inference on the Gemma 3N 4B multimodal large language model using the Unsloth library on a free Kaggle GPU. It walks through installing dependencies, loading a 4-bit quantized model, preparing conversational data from the FineTome-100k dataset, and applying LoRA adapters for efficient parameter updates. The author shows how to mask training loss to only update the model on assistant responses, then trains for a short duration before demonstrating text, image, audio, and multimodal inference. Readers learn how to export the fine-tuned model to LoRA, float16, or GGUF formats for deployment.
competition_flow: Loads a 4-bit quantized Gemma 3N 4B model, prepares conversational data from a Hugging Face dataset, fine-tunes it using LoRA and Unsloth's SFTTrainer, and demonstrates multimodal inference and model export.
data_reading: Loads the mlabonne/FineTome-100k dataset from Hugging Face using datasets.load_dataset, selecting the first 3000 training rows.
data_processing: Applies the Gemma-3 chat template to format conversations, removes the leading <bos> token, and masks the loss on user instructions during training using train_on_responses_only so gradients only update on assistant outputs.
loss_functions: Cross-entropy loss masked to only compute gradients on assistant responses (user inputs masked with -100)
insights: LoRA fine-tuning with r=8 and lora_alpha=8 efficiently adapts the model without full parameter updates.
Masking the loss to only train on model responses significantly improves fine-tuning accuracy.
4-bit quantization via Unsloth drastically reduces VRAM requirements, enabling training on free Kaggle T4 GPUs.
Gemma-3N natively supports text, vision, and audio modalities in a single unified architecture.
Recommended inference hyperparameters for Gemma-3 are temperature=1.0, top_p=0.95, and top_k=64.
